input.content: You are an enterprise assistant with access to various business tools. Use the appropriate tool to answer user queries. Always call a function when one is relevant.
Get all active requirements for version R2025x

tools.name: get_requirement_details
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information for Elementary Requirements (ER) filtered by version and other criteria. Returns individual records (or auto-aggregates if result set is large). Always returns the count.

Returns:
- id (string, pattern: ^ER\d+$): Unique identifier for the Elementary Requirement
- title (string): Title or descriptive name of the ER
- requested_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was originally requested
- planned_program (string): Program version in which the requirement is planned for delivery
- delivery_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was or will be delivered
- classification (array): Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification
- compliance_action (string): Detailed compliance status indicating specific action required
- is_compliant (boolean): Whether the ER is compliant with planning requirements
- state (string): ER lifecycle state

major_version.description: Major version indicating year of market availability (e.g., R2024x, R2025x). If omitted, returns data across all major versions.

minor_version.description: Minor version specifying release stage. GA = General Availability, FD = Functional Delivery. If omitted, returns data across all minor versions.

status.description: Business-level status aggregation for filtering ERs. 'active' = neither closed nor obsolete; 'requested' = submitted for implementation; 'planned' = assigned to specific version; 'delivered' = completed and released.

classification.description: Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification of an ER. If omitted, returns all ERs.

brand_org_name.description: Brand organization responsible for the ER. If omitted, returns all.

customer_name.description: Canonical company identifier in lowercase ASCII. Legal suffixes (Inc, Ltd, etc.) removed. Example: 'acme corp' for 'ACME Corp Inc.'

is_compliant.description: When true, returns only compliant ERs; when false, only non-compliant ERs. If omitted, returns all.

tools.name: get_requirement_roadmap
tools.description:
Retrieves the roadmap view of requirements showing planned delivery timelines across program versions. Useful for release planning and capacity management.

Returns:
- roadmap_items (array): List of requirements with their planned versions
- version_summary (object): Count of requirements per version
- timeline_view (object): Quarterly breakdown of deliveries

program_version.description: Filter by specific program version (e.g., R2025x). If omitted, returns roadmap for all versions.

time_horizon.description: Time horizon for the roadmap view. Default is 18 months.

group_by.description: How to group the roadmap items in the response.

include_delivered.description: Whether to include already delivered requirements in the roadmap.

tools.name: get_requirement_compliance
tools.description:
Retrieves compliance status and required actions for requirements. Shows which ERs need attention based on planning process compliance.

Returns:
- compliance_summary (object): Overall compliance metrics
- action_items (array): ERs requiring specific compliance actions
- non_compliant_details (array): Details of non-compliant ERs with reasons

compliance_action.description: Filter by specific compliance action required. If omitted, returns all compliance statuses.

is_compliant.description: Filter by compliance status. True = compliant only, False = non-compliant only.

tools.name: get_requirement_dependencies
tools.description:
Retrieves dependency relationships between requirements. Shows which ERs depend on others and identifies dependency chains.

Returns:
- dependencies (array): List of dependency relationships
- dependency_tree (object): Hierarchical view of dependencies
- blocking_items (array): ERs that are blocking others

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get dependencies for (e.g., ER165615). If omitted, returns all dependencies.

dependency_type.description: Type of dependency relationship to retrieve.

depth.description: How many levels deep to traverse the dependency tree. Default is 3.

include_resolved.description: Whether to include already resolved/delivered dependencies.

tools.name: get_requirement_history
tools.description:
Retrieves the change history and audit trail for requirements. Shows all modifications, status changes, and who made them.

Returns:
- history_entries (array): Chronological list of changes
- change_summary (object): Summary of change types and frequency
- audit_trail (array): Full audit log with user details

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get history for. Required for detailed history.

date_from.description: Start date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

date_to.description: End date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

changed_by.description: Filter by user who made the change (format: firstname.lastname).

tools.name: get_requirement_metrics
tools.description:
Retrieves KPIs and progress metrics for requirements. Shows delivery rates, compliance percentages, and trend analysis.

Returns:
- delivery_metrics (object): On-time delivery rates, average cycle time
- compliance_metrics (object): Compliance percentages by category
- trend_data (array): Historical trends for key metrics

tools.name: search_requirements
tools.description:
Full-text search across requirements with advanced filtering. Searches titles, descriptions, comments, and linked documents.

Returns:
- results (array): Matching requirements with relevance scores
- facets (object): Aggregated counts by status, classification, etc.
- total_count (integer): Total number of matches

search_query.description: Full-text search query. Supports AND, OR, NOT operators and phrase matching with quotes.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: get_requirement_assignments
tools.description:
Retrieves team and owner assignments for requirements. Shows who is responsible for each ER and workload distribution.

Returns:
- assignments (array): List of requirements with their assignees
- workload_summary (object): Workload per team/individual
- unassigned (array): Requirements without owners

tools.name: get_requirement_comments
tools.description:
Retrieves discussion threads and comments on requirements. Shows all stakeholder communications and decisions.

Returns:
- comments (array): List of comments with author and timestamp
- threads (array): Grouped discussion threads
- mentions (array): Comments that mention specific users

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get comments for.

since_date.description: Get comments since this date (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

tools.name: get_requirement_attachments
tools.description:
Retrieves documents and files attached to requirements. Shows specifications, designs, and supporting materials.

Returns:
- attachments (array): List of attached files with metadata
- linked_documents (array): References to external documents
- total_size (integer): Total size of attachments in bytes

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get attachments for.

uploaded_after.description: Get files uploaded after this date.

tools.name: get_project_details
tools.description:
Retrieves comprehensive project information including metadata, status, and key attributes. Returns project profile with all associated data.

Returns:

- name (string): Project name
- phase (string): Current project phase
- status (string): Project health status
- budget (object): Budget information
- timeline (object): Start/end dates and milestones

project_id.description: Unique project identifier (format: PRJ-123456). Required for single project lookup.

status.description: Filter by project health status (RAG status).

tools.name: get_project_milestones
tools.description:

tools.name: get_project_timeline
tools.description:

tools.name: get_project_deliverables
tools.description:

Returns:

tools.name: search_projects
tools.description:
Full-text search across project portfolio with advanced filtering.

Returns:

- facets (object): Aggregations by status, phase, portfolio
- total_count (integer): Total matches

tools.name: get_project_dependencies
tools.description:
Retrieves inter-project dependencies showing which projects depend on others.

tools.name: get_change_request
tools.description:

Returns:

tools.name: get_requirement_details
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information for Elementary Requirements (ER) filtered by version and other criteria. Returns individual records (or auto-aggregates if result set is large). Always returns the count.

Returns:
- id (string, pattern: ^ER\d+$): Unique identifier for the Elementary Requirement
- title (string): Title or descriptive name of the ER
- requested_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was originally requested
- planned_program (string): Program version in which the requirement is planned for delivery
- delivery_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was or will be delivered
- classification (array): Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification
- compliance_action (string): Detailed compliance status indicating specific action required
- is_compliant (boolean): Whether the ER is compliant with planning requirements
- state (string): ER lifecycle state

major_version.description: Major version indicating year of market availability (e.g., R2024x, R2025x). If omitted, returns data across all major versions.

minor_version.description: Minor version specifying release stage. GA = General Availability, FD = Functional Delivery. If omitted, returns data across all minor versions.

status.description: Business-level status aggregation for filtering ERs. 'active' = neither closed nor obsolete; 'requested' = submitted for implementation; 'planned' = assigned to specific version; 'delivered' = completed and released.

classification.description: Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification of an ER. If omitted, returns all ERs.

brand_org_name.description: Brand organization responsible for the ER. If omitted, returns all.

customer_name.description: Canonical company identifier in lowercase ASCII. Legal suffixes (Inc, Ltd, etc.) removed. Example: 'acme corp' for 'ACME Corp Inc.'

is_compliant.description: When true, returns only compliant ERs; when false, only non-compliant ERs. If omitted, returns all.

tools.name: get_requirement_roadmap
tools.description:
Retrieves the roadmap view of requirements showing planned delivery timelines across program versions. Useful for release planning and capacity management.

Returns:
- roadmap_items (array): List of requirements with their planned versions
- version_summary (object): Count of requirements per version
- timeline_view (object): Quarterly breakdown of deliveries

program_version.description: Filter by specific program version (e.g., R2025x). If omitted, returns roadmap for all versions.

time_horizon.description: Time horizon for the roadmap view. Default is 18 months.

group_by.description: How to group the roadmap items in the response.

include_delivered.description: Whether to include already delivered requirements in the roadmap.

tools.name: get_requirement_compliance

Returns:
- compliance_summary (object): Overall compliance metrics
- action_items (array): ERs requiring specific compliance actions
- non_compliant_details (array): Details of non-compliant ERs with reasons

compliance_action.description: Filter by specific compliance action required. If omitted, returns all compliance statuses.

is_compliant.description: Filter by compliance status. True = compliant only, False = non-compliant only.

tools.name: get_requirement_dependencies
tools.description:
Retrieves dependency relationships between requirements. Shows which ERs depend on others and identifies dependency chains.

Returns:
- dependencies (array): List of dependency relationships
- dependency_tree (object): Hierarchical view of dependencies
- blocking_items (array): ERs that are blocking others

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get dependencies for (e.g., ER165615). If omitted, returns all dependencies.

dependency_type.description: Type of dependency relationship to retrieve.

depth.description: How many levels deep to traverse the dependency tree. Default is 3.

include_resolved.description: Whether to include already resolved/delivered dependencies.

tools.name: get_requirement_history

Retrieves the change history and audit trail for requirements. Shows all modifications, status changes, and who made them.

Returns:

- audit_trail (array): Full audit log with user details

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get history for. Required for detailed history.

date_from.description: Start date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

date_to.description: End date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

changed_by.description: Filter by user who made the change (format: firstname.lastname).

search_query.description: Full-text search query. Supports AND, OR, NOT operators and phrase matching with quotes.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: get_requirement_details
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information for Elementary Requirements (ER) filtered by version and other criteria. Returns individual records (or auto-aggregates if result set is large). Always returns the count.

Returns:
- id (string, pattern: ^ER\d+$): Unique identifier for the Elementary Requirement
- title (string): Title or descriptive name of the ER
- requested_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was originally requested
- planned_program (string): Program version in which the requirement is planned for delivery
- delivery_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was or will be delivered
- classification (array): Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification
- compliance_action (string): Detailed compliance status indicating specific action required
- is_compliant (boolean): Whether the ER is compliant with planning requirements
- state (string): ER lifecycle state

major_version.description: Major version indicating year of market availability (e.g., R2024x, R2025x). If omitted, returns data across all major versions.

minor_version.description: Minor version specifying release stage. GA = General Availability, FD = Functional Delivery. If omitted, returns data across all minor versions.

status.description: Business-level status aggregation for filtering ERs. 'active' = neither closed nor obsolete; 'requested' = submitted for implementation; 'planned' = assigned to specific version; 'delivered' = completed and released.

classification.description: Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification of an ER. If omitted, returns all ERs.

brand_org_name.description: Brand organization responsible for the ER. If omitted, returns all.

customer_name.description: Canonical company identifier in lowercase ASCII. Legal suffixes (Inc, Ltd, etc.) removed. Example: 'acme corp' for 'ACME Corp Inc.'

is_compliant.description: When true, returns only compliant ERs; when false, only non-compliant ERs. If omitted, returns all.

tools.name: get_requirement_roadmap
tools.description:
Retrieves the roadmap view of requirements showing planned delivery timelines across program versions. Useful for release planning and capacity management.

Returns:
- roadmap_items (array): List of requirements with their planned versions
- version_summary (object): Count of requirements per version
- timeline_view (object): Quarterly breakdown of deliveries

program_version.description: Filter by specific program version (e.g., R2025x). If omitted, returns roadmap for all versions.

time_horizon.description: Time horizon for the roadmap view. Default is 18 months.

group_by.description: How to group the roadmap items in the response.

include_delivered.description: Whether to include already delivered requirements in the roadmap.

Returns:

- non_compliant_details (array): Details of non-compliant ERs with reasons

compliance_action.description: Filter by specific compliance action required. If omitted, returns all compliance statuses.

is_compliant.description: Filter by compliance status. True = compliant only, False = non-compliant only.

tools.name: get_requirement_dependencies
tools.description:
Retrieves dependency relationships between requirements. Shows which ERs depend on others and identifies dependency chains.

Returns:
- dependencies (array): List of dependency relationships
- dependency_tree (object): Hierarchical view of dependencies
- blocking_items (array): ERs that are blocking others

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get dependencies for (e.g., ER165615). If omitted, returns all dependencies.

dependency_type.description: Type of dependency relationship to retrieve.

depth.description: How many levels deep to traverse the dependency tree. Default is 3.

include_resolved.description: Whether to include already resolved/delivered dependencies.

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get history for. Required for detailed history.

date_from.description: Start date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

date_to.description: End date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

changed_by.description: Filter by user who made the change (format: firstname.lastname).

tools.name: get_requirement_details
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information for Elementary Requirements (ER) filtered by version and other criteria. Returns individual records (or auto-aggregates if result set is large). Always returns the count.

Returns:
- id (string, pattern: ^ER\d+$): Unique identifier for the Elementary Requirement
- title (string): Title or descriptive name of the ER
- requested_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was originally requested
- planned_program (string): Program version in which the requirement is planned for delivery
- delivery_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was or will be delivered
- classification (array): Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification
- compliance_action (string): Detailed compliance status indicating specific action required
- is_compliant (boolean): Whether the ER is compliant with planning requirements
- state (string): ER lifecycle state

major_version.description: Major version indicating year of market availability (e.g., R2024x, R2025x). If omitted, returns data across all major versions.

minor_version.description: Minor version specifying release stage. GA = General Availability, FD = Functional Delivery. If omitted, returns data across all minor versions.

status.description: Business-level status aggregation for filtering ERs. 'active' = neither closed nor obsolete; 'requested' = submitted for implementation; 'planned' = assigned to specific version; 'delivered' = completed and released.

classification.description: Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification of an ER. If omitted, returns all ERs.

brand_org_name.description: Brand organization responsible for the ER. If omitted, returns all.

customer_name.description: Canonical company identifier in lowercase ASCII. Legal suffixes (Inc, Ltd, etc.) removed. Example: 'acme corp' for 'ACME Corp Inc.'

is_compliant.description: When true, returns only compliant ERs; when false, only non-compliant ERs. If omitted, returns all.

tools.name: get_requirement_roadmap

- version_summary (object): Count of requirements per version
- timeline_view (object): Quarterly breakdown of deliveries

program_version.description: Filter by specific program version (e.g., R2025x). If omitted, returns roadmap for all versions.

time_horizon.description: Time horizon for the roadmap view. Default is 18 months.

group_by.description: How to group the roadmap items in the response.

include_delivered.description: Whether to include already delivered requirements in the roadmap.

compliance_action.description: Filter by specific compliance action required. If omitted, returns all compliance statuses.

is_compliant.description: Filter by compliance status. True = compliant only, False = non-compliant only.

tools.name: get_requirement_dependencies

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get dependencies for (e.g., ER165615). If omitted, returns all dependencies.

dependency_type.description: Type of dependency relationship to retrieve.

depth.description: How many levels deep to traverse the dependency tree. Default is 3.

include_resolved.description: Whether to include already resolved/delivered dependencies.

date_from.description: Start date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

tools.name: get_requirement_details
tools.description:
Retrieves detailed information for Elementary Requirements (ER) filtered by version and other criteria. Returns individual records (or auto-aggregates if result set is large). Always returns the count.

Returns:
- id (string, pattern: ^ER\d+$): Unique identifier for the Elementary Requirement
- title (string): Title or descriptive name of the ER
- requested_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was originally requested
- planned_program (string): Program version in which the requirement is planned for delivery
- delivery_program (string): Program version in which the requirement was or will be delivered
- classification (array): Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification
- compliance_action (string): Detailed compliance status indicating specific action required
- is_compliant (boolean): Whether the ER is compliant with planning requirements
- state (string): ER lifecycle state

major_version.description: Major version indicating year of market availability (e.g., R2024x, R2025x). If omitted, returns data across all major versions.

minor_version.description: Minor version specifying release stage. GA = General Availability, FD = Functional Delivery. If omitted, returns data across all minor versions.

status.description: Business-level status aggregation for filtering ERs. 'active' = neither closed nor obsolete; 'requested' = submitted for implementation; 'planned' = assigned to specific version; 'delivered' = completed and released.

classification.description: Strategic, technical, or customer-related classification of an ER. If omitted, returns all ERs.

brand_org_name.description: Brand organization responsible for the ER. If omitted, returns all.

customer_name.description: Canonical company identifier in lowercase ASCII. Legal suffixes (Inc, Ltd, etc.) removed. Example: 'acme corp' for 'ACME Corp Inc.'

is_compliant.description: When true, returns only compliant ERs; when false, only non-compliant ERs. If omitted, returns all.

tools.name: get_requirement_roadmap
tools.description:

Returns:
- roadmap_items (array): List of requirements with their planned versions
- version_summary (object): Count of requirements per version
- timeline_view (object): Quarterly breakdown of deliveries

program_version.description: Filter by specific program version (e.g., R2025x). If omitted, returns roadmap for all versions.

time_horizon.description: Time horizon for the roadmap view. Default is 18 months.

group_by.description: How to group the roadmap items in the response.

include_delivered.description: Whether to include already delivered requirements in the roadmap.

tools.name: get_requirement_compliance

Returns:
- compliance_summary (object): Overall compliance metrics
- action_items (array): ERs requiring specific compliance actions
- non_compliant_details (array): Details of non-compliant ERs with reasons

compliance_action.description: Filter by specific compliance action required. If omitted, returns all compliance statuses.

is_compliant.description: Filter by compliance status. True = compliant only, False = non-compliant only.

tools.name: get_requirement_dependencies
tools.description:

Returns:
- dependencies (array): List of dependency relationships
- dependency_tree (object): Hierarchical view of dependencies
- blocking_items (array): ERs that are blocking others

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get dependencies for (e.g., ER165615). If omitted, returns all dependencies.

dependency_type.description: Type of dependency relationship to retrieve.

depth.description: How many levels deep to traverse the dependency tree. Default is 3.

include_resolved.description: Whether to include already resolved/delivered dependencies.

tools.name: get_requirement_history

Returns:

requirement_id.description: Specific ER ID to get history for. Required for detailed history.

date_from.description: Start date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

date_to.description: End date for history range (ISO format: YYYY-MM-DD).

changed_by.description: Filter by user who made the change (format: firstname.lastname).

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: get_team_composition
tools.description:
Retrieves team structure and members.

Returns:
- team (object): Team information
- members (array): Team members
- org_chart (object): Organizational structure

tools.name: get_workforce_capacity
tools.description:
Retrieves workforce capacity and availability.

Returns:
- capacity (object): Total capacity
- allocated (object): Currently allocated
- available (object): Available capacity

tools.name: get_workforce_forecasts
tools.description:
Retrieves workforce demand forecasts and planning.

Returns:
- forecasts (array): Demand forecasts
- gaps (array): Capacity gaps
- recommendations (array): Staffing recommendations

tools.name: get_employee_availability
tools.description:
Retrieves employee availability calendar.

Returns:
- availability (array): Availability by date
- time_off (array): Scheduled time off
- conflicts (array): Scheduling conflicts
